name: DURHAM, JOHNSTONE SCHOOL RH9

surface_od_m.qualifier: absent — no Ground Level (m AOD) box; depth column datum is G.L. (ground level)

units: metres below ground level, transcribed verbatim from typed log — depth = base of interval

annotations: thin 0.10 m coal band within the sand-dominated drift; no seam name given in original
description reads only 'Coal Traces'; cf. RH8's parallel basal entry 'Sand / Coal Traces' — interpreted as sand with disseminated coal traces within the drift, not a coal seam; classed drift

notes: Page 1 is a typed multi-hole strata sheet (job no. 950197) listing three rotary open holes RH2, RH8 and RH9 in a single Depth(m)/Description table; only RH9 is transcribed in this record. Header title reads 'DURHAM JOHNSTON SCHOOL, WHINNEY HILL, DURHAM, CO. DURHAM' — spells the school 'JOHNSTON' (no final E), whereas the BGS reference/name supplied for this hole is 'JOHNSTONE'; task-supplied name kept. No Ground Level (m AOD) datum given; depths are below G.L. Per the page-2 key this is a rotary open hole; the full 30.00 m is glacial drift (boulder clay over sand) with no bedrock rockhead proved — the thin 0.10 m 'Coal' (12.20-12.30) and the basal 'Coal Traces' (23.45-30.00) sit within the sand. Depth column is fully self-consistent, no clerk arithmetic slips. Page 2 is the 'Borehole Location Plan — Durham Johnston School' (HJT Solmek, drawing no. 950197/L1, dated FEB. 96, drawn A.C., checked D.B.), a location plan only with no strata.
